ask_next.question: How long has the seizure been going on?
ask_next.purpose: Detect status epilepticus (seizure lasting over 5 minutes)
ask_next.why: Seizures >5 minutes = status epilepticus, a life-threatening neurological emergency

ask_next.question: Has this person had seizures before, or is this the first time?
ask_next.purpose: Determine if known epileptic with a rescue medication plan
ask_next.why: First seizures always require 911; known epileptics may have Diastat or other rescue medication

response_guides.note: First or unknown seizure — call 911 regardless of duration
Known epileptic — follow their seizure action plan if available, call 911 if >5 min or unusual

ask_next.question: Is the person breathing now that the shaking has stopped?
ask_next.why: Respiratory arrest can follow tonic-clonic seizures and requires immediate CPR

response_guides.note: Respiratory arrest post-seizure — begin CPR immediately, call 911
